div1.entryFree:
Test, subst. proof; 1) trial, examination: thou hast strangely stood the t. Tp. IV, 7. let there be some more t. made of my metal, Meas. I, 1, 49. bring me to the t. Hml. III, 4, 142.
2) testimony, evidence: an esperance so obstinately strong, that doth invert that t. of eyes and ears, Troil. V, 2, 122 (Q the attest). to vouch this is no proof, without more wider and more overt t. Oth. I, 3, 107.
